title: Simplify complex fractions
topic_path: Topic B: Multiplication and division of integers and rational numbers

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression is equivalent to the following complex fraction?**

> $\Large\dfrac{ ~\frac {2}{-6}~ }{ \frac 3{-2} }$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The correct answer is "None of the above."

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression is equivalent to the following complex fraction?**

> $\Large\dfrac{ ~\frac {a}{b}~ }{c}$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: So, $\Large \dfrac{ ~\blue{\frac {a}{b}}~ }{ \pink c }$ is equivalent to $\blue{\dfrac {a}{b}} \div \pink c$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression is equivalent to the following complex fraction?**

> $\Large\dfrac{ ~\frac {-5}{2}~ }{\frac{3}{8}}$

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which expression is equivalent to the following complex fraction?**

> $\Large\dfrac{ ~\frac {7}{-3}~ }{-2}$

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which expression is equivalent to the following complex fraction?**

> $\Large\dfrac{ ~\frac {2}{-b}~ }{\frac{4}{7}}$

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which expression is equivalent to the following complex fraction?**

> $\Large\dfrac{ ~-\frac {w}{x}~ }{\frac{y}{-z}}$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The correct answer is "None of the above."

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression is equivalent to the following complex fraction?**

> $\Large\dfrac{ ~\frac {-a}{-b}~ }{\frac{c}{d}}$

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which expression is equivalent to the following complex fraction?**

> $\Large\dfrac{ ~\normalsize2~ }{ \frac {4}{5} }$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To divide by a fraction, we multiply by the reciprocal of the fraction.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression is equivalent to the following complex fraction?**

> $\Large\dfrac{ ~\frac {-5}{x}~ }{ \frac 1{4} }$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To divide by a fraction, we multiply by the reciprocal of the fraction.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression is equivalent to the following complex fraction?**

> $\Large\dfrac{ ~\frac {d}{4}~ }{ \frac {3}{d} }$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To divide by a fraction, we multiply by the reciprocal of the fraction.
$\dfrac d4 \cdot \dfrac  d3$ is the correct answer.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression is equivalent to the following complex fraction?**

> $\Large\dfrac{ ~\normalsize{x}~ }{ \frac {4}{7} }$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To divide by a fraction, we multiply by the reciprocal of the fraction.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression is equivalent to the following complex fraction?**

> $\Large\dfrac{ ~\frac {-j}{k}~ }{ \frac {-l}{m} }$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To divide by a fraction, we multiply by the reciprocal of the fraction.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression is equivalent to the following complex fraction?**

> $\Large\dfrac{ ~~\frac {7}{4}~~ }{ -\frac {9}{8} }$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To divide by a fraction, we multiply by the reciprocal of the fraction.


${\dfrac {7}{4} }\div \left({-\dfrac 9{8}}\right) = \dfrac74 \cdot \left(-\dfrac89\right)$
"None of the above" is the correct answer.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expression is equivalent to the following complex fraction?**

> $\Large\dfrac{ ~\frac {4}{r}~ }{ \frac {2}{-s} }$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To divide by a fraction, we multiply by the reciprocal of the fraction.


${\dfrac {4}{r} }\div \dfrac 2{-s} = \dfrac 4r \cdot \dfrac{-s}2$
"None of the above" is the correct answer.

assessment_items.question_content:
Claud tried to evaluate an expression step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
&\Large\dfrac{ ~\frac{-3}{4}~ }{ \frac5{-6}} \\\\
\\
=&\dfrac{-3}{4}\div \dfrac{5}{-6}&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
=&\dfrac{-3}{4}\cdot \dfrac{-6}{5}&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\\
=&\:\:\:\dfrac{9}{10}&\purple{\text{Step } 3} \\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Claud's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ correctly changed from a complex fraction to a division problem.
$\blue{\text{Step }2}$ correctly changed from a division problem to multiplying by the reciprocal.
Claud did not make a mistake.

choices.content: Claud did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Russell tried to evaluate an expression step by step.

$\begin{align}
&\Large\dfrac{ ~\frac{5}{11}~ }{ \frac{-3}{5}} \\\\
=&\dfrac{5}{11}\div \dfrac{-3}{5}&\greenD{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
=&\dfrac{5}{11}\cdot \dfrac{5}{3}&\blueD{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
=&\dfrac{25}{33}&\purpleC{\text{Step } 3} 
\end{align}$

**Find Russell's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\greenD{\text{Step }1}$ correctly changed from a complex fraction to dividing the top fraction by the bottom fraction.
$\blueD{\text{Step }2}$ changed from a division problem to multiplying by the opposite of the reciprocal.

This is a mistake! 

Russell should be evaluating $\dfrac{5}{11}\cdot \dfrac{5}{-3}$, not $\dfrac{5}{11}\cdot \dfrac{5}{\redD{3}}$.

choices.content: Russell did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Wilson tried to evaluate an expression step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
&\Large\dfrac{ ~\frac{3}{-2}~ }{ \frac{9}{-4}} \\\\
\\
=&\dfrac{9}{-4}\div \dfrac{3}{-2}&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
=&\dfrac{9}{-4}\cdot \dfrac{-2}{3}&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\\
=&\:\:\:\dfrac{3}{2}&\purple{\text{Step } 3} \\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Wilson's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\green{\text{Step }1}$ changed from a complex fraction to dividing the bottom fraction by the top fraction.
This is a mistake! 

Wilson should be evaluating $\dfrac{3}{-2}\div \dfrac{9}{-4}$, not $\red{\dfrac{9}{-4}\div \dfrac{3}{-2}}$.

Wilson made a mistake in $\green{\text{Step }1}$.

choices.content: Wilson did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Peyton tried to evaluate an expression step by step.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\Large\dfrac{ ~\frac{a}{b}~ }{ \frac{-c}{d}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{a}{b}\div \dfrac{-c}{d}&\greenD{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
&=\dfrac{a}{b}\cdot \dfrac{d}{-c}&\blueD{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
&=-\dfrac{ad}{-bc}&\purpleC{\text{Step } 3} 
\end{align}$

**Find Peyton's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\greenD{\text{Step }1}$ correctly changed from a complex fraction to a division problem.
$\blueD{\text{Step }2}$ correctly changed from a division problem to multiplying by the reciprocal.

This is a mistake! 
Peyton should have gotten $-\dfrac{ad}{bc}$, not $\redD{-\dfrac{ad}{-bc}}$.

choices.content: Peyton did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Eva tried to evaluate an expression step by step.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\Large\dfrac{ ~\frac{-7}{-2}~ }{ \frac{4}{9}} \\\\
&=\dfrac{-7}{-2}\div \dfrac{4}{9}&\greenD{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
&=\dfrac{-7}{-2}\cdot \dfrac{9}{4}&\blueD{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
&=-\dfrac{63}{8}&\purpleC{\text{Step } 3} 
\end{align}$

**Find Eva's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\greenD{\text{Step }1}$ correctly changed from a complex fraction to a division problem.
$\blueD{\text{Step }2}$ correctly changed from a division problem to multiplying by the reciprocal.

This is a mistake! 
Eva should have gotten $\dfrac{63}{8}$, not $\redD{-\dfrac{63}{8}}$.

choices.content: Eva did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Li tried to evaluate an expression step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
&\Large\dfrac{ ~\frac{-4}{5}~ }{ \frac{8}{-3}} \\\\
\\
=&\dfrac{-4}{5}\div \dfrac{8}{-3}&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
=&\dfrac{-4}{5}\cdot \dfrac{-3}{8}&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\\
=&\:\:\:-\dfrac{3}{10}&\purple{\text{Step } 3} \\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Li's mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ correctly changed from a complex fraction to a division problem.
$\blue{\text{Step }2}$ correctly changed from a division problem to multiplying by the reciprocal.

This is a mistake! 

Li should have gotten $\dfrac{3}{10}$, not $\red-\dfrac{3}{10}$.

choices.content: Li did not make a mistake.

assessment_items.question_content:
Zhang tried to evaluate an expression step by step.

$\qquad\begin{align}
&\Large\dfrac{ ~\frac{-x}{3}~ }{ \frac{2}{y}} \\\\
\\
=&\dfrac{-x}{3}\div \dfrac{2}{y}&\green{\text{Step } 1}\\\\
\\
=&\dfrac{-x}{3}\cdot \dfrac{y}{2}&\blue{\text{Step } 2}\\\\
\\
=&\:\:\:-\dfrac{xy}{6}&\purple{\text{Step } 3} \\\\
\end{align}$

**Find Zhang's  mistake.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\green{\text{Step }1}$ correctly changed from a complex fraction to a division problem.
$\blue{\text{Step }2}$ correctly changed from a division problem to multiplying by the reciprocal.
Zhang did not make a mistake.

choices.content: Zhang did not make a mistake.
